summary_draft: Polish cryptocurrency exchange that shut down in April 2019 after earlier KNF regulatory action; customer-fund loss allegations exist, but this stub keeps the terminal cause conservative until stronger primary evidence is secured.

non_archive.title: Polish Cryptocurrency Exchange Shuts Down Overnight, Taking Funds With It
non_archive.purpose: shutdown timing / overnight closure / user loss reporting

non_archive.title: Report: Polish Exchange Shuts Down and Disappears With Customers Funds
non_archive.purpose: secondary corroboration of shutdown and fund-loss allegations

evidence_notes: Do not force scam_rug at this stage.
Shutdown is strong enough for dead-side handling, but the primary cause remains mixed across regulatory action and later customer-loss allegations.
Need archive plus at least two non-archive sources before public_ready promotion.

event_candidates.title: KNF public warning entry
event_candidates.description: Coinroom was added to the Polish KNF public warning list over suspected unlicensed payment-services activity.

event_candidates.title: Coinroom shutdown
event_candidates.description: Users reported receiving account-termination notices and the exchange ceased operating in early April 2019.

risks: stronger official closure notice still desirable
final_notes: Keep Coinroom as dead / unknown for now.
Next step: find stronger archived official terms, notices, or preserved emails around the April 2019 shutdown.
